topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the login details tucked inside a forgotten charging cable in Liam’s backpack. My heart began to hammer against my ribs as I typed them in. The screen flickered, and suddenly, a feed of cryptic posts, carefully curated photos, and interactions with hundreds of unknown users flooded the display. My breath hitched. I stared, frozen, at the screen, my hands trembling so badly I almost dropped the phone. It wasn't just a secret; it was an entire, meticulously constructed digital life I had never known existed. A wave of profound disorientation washed over me, leaving a cold, hollow feeling in my chest.

[story 2]

When Sarah discovered the hidden profile, she physically recoiled, stumbling back from the laptop. Her knuckles were white where she gripped the edge of the desk. Her eyes darted rapidly between the screen and her son, who was engrossed in a game nearby, oblivious. A slow, shallow intake of air escaped her lungs. She slowly leaned closer, her gaze fixed on the profile picture—a filtered, distant version of her teenager. A strange tension coiled in her stomach, tightening until she felt physically unable to move. Her voice, when it finally emerged, was a thin, reedy whisper.

[story 3]

I watched the notifications scroll by, a dizzying cascade of likes and comments that seemed to mock my reality. My jaw went slack. I felt a sudden, sharp tightness across my forehead, and I instinctively reached up to press my fingers against my temples. Every muscle in my body seemed to lock up. The silence in the room amplified the sound of my own rapid, uneven breathing. I stood there for a long moment, just observing the illuminated screen, a profound sense of being utterly unprepared settling over me like a heavy blanket.
